name: Monzón de Campos Castle and Canal de Castilla Loop
name_original: Paseo de los Castrillones – circular desde Monzón de Campos por Valdespina
description: Cycle the moderate 19.6-mile Paseo de los Castrillones loop from Monzón de Campos, exploring Palencia's varied terrain and history.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Monzón De Campos features routes primarily across the flat to gently rolling plains of Castilla y León. The landscape is characterized by agricultural fields, historical villages, and the notable Canal de Castilla, which provides numerous flat, scenic paths. Elevation changes are generally minimal, making the area suitable for various cycling abilities. The region offers a mix of open countryside and routes alongside waterways. Best touring cycling routes around Monzón De Campos The most popular touring cycling route is Monzón de Campos Castle and Canal de Castilla Loop, a 19.6 miles (31.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 26 minutes to complete. This moderate route combines historical landmarks with scenic canal-side riding. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is San Cebrián de Campos and Man, a moderate 13.0 miles (20.9 km) path. This route offers views of rural landscapes and agricultural areas. Local touring cyclists also love the Frómista – Calahorra de Ribas Locks loop from Becerril de Campos, a 48.5 miles (78.1 km) trail leading through the Canal de Castilla region, often completed in about 7 hours 3 minutes. Touring cycling around Monzón De Campos is defined by canal paths, open agricultural plains, and historical village surroundings. The network offers options for easy touring bike trails as well as more challenging long-distance bike routes. The routes in Monzón De Campos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Monzón De Campos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Monzón De Campos?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes in the Monzón De Campos area, offering a variety of options for different skill levels. You'll find paths ranging from easy, flat rides along the Canal de Castilla to more challenging, longer-distance routes across the plains.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring bike routes in Monzón De Campos?
A: The terrain around Monzón De Campos is primarily flat to gently rolling, characteristic of the Castilla y León plains. Many routes follow the historic Canal de Castilla, providing smooth, level paths. You'll cycle through vast agricultural fields and past charming historical villages, with minimal elevation changes overall.

Q: Are there any easy touring bike trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Monzón De Campos offers several easy touring bike trails perfect for beginners or families. These routes often utilize the flat towpaths alongside the Canal de Castilla. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1175519652/san-cebrian-de-campos-and-man" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Cebrián de Campos and Man&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 13.0-mile (20.9 km) path offering pleasant rural views.

Q: Can I find longer, more challenging touring bike routes in the area?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a greater challenge, there are several long-distance routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40011991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Frómista – Calahorra de Ribas Locks loop from Becerril de Campos&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 48.5-mile (78.1 km) trail that explores the Canal de Castilla region and typically takes around 7 hours to complete.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I might see while cycling?
A: Many routes in the region offer glimpses of historical and natural landmarks. The Canal de Castilla itself is a major feature, with its impressive locks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2615872" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calahorra de Ribas Locks&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass through historical villages and see traditional agricultural landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1170901608/monzon-de-campos-castle-and-canal-de-castilla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monzón de Campos Castle and Canal de Castilla Loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically combines historical sights with canal-side riding.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Monzón De Campos?
A: The most pleasant times for touring cycling in Monzón De Campos are generally spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is typically mild, and the landscapes are vibrant. Summers can be quite hot, and winters can be chilly, though cycling is possible year-round with appropriate gear.

Q: Are there any circular touring bike routes available?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Monzón De Campos are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40314088" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla – Casa Grande de Palencia loop from Grijota&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 33-mile (53 km) circular route that offers a comprehensive experience of the canal and surrounding areas.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Monzón De Campos?
A: The touring cycling routes in Monzón De Campos are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the peaceful canal paths, the flat and accessible terrain, and the opportunity to explore the historical and rural charm of the region.

Q: Where can I typically find parking for touring bike routes?
A: Given the rural nature of the area, parking is generally available in the villages that serve as starting points for many routes. For routes along the Canal de Castilla, you can often find parking near access points or in nearby towns like Monzón de Campos itself, or other villages mentioned in route descriptions.

Q: Are there any routes that offer a good mix of natural scenery and historical elements?
A: Many routes in Monzón De Campos blend natural beauty with historical interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1170901608/monzon-de-campos-castle-and-canal-de-castilla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monzón de Campos Castle and Canal de Castilla Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice, combining the scenic paths along the Canal de Castilla with views of historical structures, including the castle.

Q: What are the general weather conditions like for cycling in Monzón De Campos?
A: Monzón De Campos experiences a continental climate. Summers are typically hot and dry, while winters can be cold with occasional frost. Spring and autumn offer mild temperatures, making them ideal for cycling. It's always advisable to check the local forecast before heading out, especially for longer rides.
tips: Palencia fortification located at the top of a hill where it could dominate the surrounding plain.
Valdespina is a small and charming town located in the province of Palencia. With its calm atmosphere and rural setting, it is a perfect place to enjoy the natural beauty.

It is undoubtedly one of the most spectacular sets of locks of all those that make up the Canal de Castilla.
Undoubtedly one of the most photogenic enclaves.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Paseo de los Castrillones cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route features varied terrain. While some sections are paved, you should expect significant unpaved segments that can be challenging. A touring bicycle capable of handling gravel or compacted dirt surfaces is highly recommended for this moderate ride.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along this cycling route?
faqs.answer: As you cycle, you'll pass by several notable features. These include the historic Monzón de Campos Castle, the charming settlement of Valdespina, and the impressive Calahorra de Ribas Locks.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Palencia region, where this route is located, offers beautiful natural landscapes. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant weather for cycling, with milder temperatures and less chance of extreme heat or cold. Summer can be enjoyable but be prepared for warmer conditions.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one starting and ending in Monzón de Campos. You can typically find parking within the town of Monzón de Campos to begin your ride conveniently.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Paseo de los Castrillones route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Is the Paseo de los Castrillones route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through natural areas and settlements, specific regulations regarding dogs are not detailed. If bringing a dog, it's generally advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in populated areas or near livestock, and to ensure they are comfortable with varied terrain and the distance.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This moderate touring bicycle route is approximately 31.5 km long and is estimated to take around 2 hours and 26 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other official trails for short segments. These include sections of the Canal de Castilla (Ramal Norte-Sur), GR-89 BTT Canal de Castilla, Camino de la Parra, and Camino de Calahorra.

faqs.question: What type of bicycle is best suited for this route?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain, which includes unpaved sections, a touring bicycle with good tires capable of handling gravel or compacted dirt is most suitable. A road bike might struggle on some segments.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through the settlement of Valdespina and starts/ends in Monzón de Campos. You may find cafes, pubs, or other establishments in these towns to refuel or refresh.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while cycling?
faqs.answer: You can expect a diverse visual experience as the route traverses the province of Palencia. The landscape transitions from the plains of Tierra de Campos towards more mountainous areas, offering a blend of natural beauty, historical elements, and cultural sights.
